description: AA-10.1 claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema and artifact bundle contract for runtime ascension closure.

graph_schema.description: Minimal graph linking runtime claims to their supporting evidence, governing policies, and replay traces.

node_types.description: A verifiable assertion about runtime behavior (e.g., 'p99 scheduling latency <= 500us').
An artifact or measurement that supports or refutes a claim.
A rule governing claim evaluation, promotion, or rejection.
A replayable execution trace linked to a workload.
A reproducible workload from the replay corpus.
A deterministic test that produces evidence.
A rollback trigger linked to a claim violation.

bundle_contract.description: An artifact bundle packages claims, evidence, policies, and traces for validation.

validation_rules.description: Every evidenced or verified claim must have at least one SUPPORTS edge.
All edge endpoints must reference existing nodes.
Every safety claim must be governed by at least one mandatory policy.
Every rollback must have a non-empty command.
Evidence of kind test_result should have a PRODUCES edge from a TEST.
Every trace must REPLAYS exactly one workload.

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate graph schema node and edge type definitions
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract schema -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate bundle contract and validation rules
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract bundle -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate graph validation logic and rejection reporting
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract validation -- --nocapture
